latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
26 Jun 2026 17:09
RNS Number : 0346K
Devolver Digital, Inc.
26 June 2026
26 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Result of AGM
Devolver Digital (AIM: DEVO), an award-winning digital publisher and developer of indie video games, is pleased to announce that
all resolutions proposed at the Annual Meeting of the Company held on Friday 26 June 2026 were duly passed.
The full text of the resolutions can be found in the Notice of Annual Meeting, which is available on the Company's website at
https://investors.devolverdigital.com
The full proxy results will be available shortly on the Company's website at
https://investors.devolverdigital.com
.
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 150 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next three years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

latest.title: Trading Statement
latest.content:
26 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8529J
Devolver Digital, Inc.
26 June 2026
26 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Trading Statement
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("indie") video games,
provides an update on current financial performance for the half year ending 30 June 2026 ("1H 2026") and outlook for full year 2026.
1H 2026 performance has been ahead of expectations with a very strong revenue performance expected to be at least 60% higher than 1H 2025, with underlying Adjusted EBITDA projected to reach mid single-digit US$ millions. The favourable year-on-year comparison is in part due to the Devolver Steam Publisher sale being held in 1H 2026, in addition to a positive start to the year with 3 Top 10 Global Best Sellers on Steam in January.
In June, Devolver's subsidiary System Era released
STARSEEKER: Astroneer Expeditions
into Early Access with a significant platform deal accompanying the launch. Unit sales have been disappointing so far but review scores are improving and there are regular content updates planned for August and through to the end of 2026 which the Board believes can re-energise the game. Separately, we are encouraged by our strong lineup in the second half of the year. Subsidiary studio Firefly enjoyed a very positive response to its announcement of
Stronghold 4,
building 250,000 wishlists in just two weeks. Equally encouraging is the interest in other games slated for 2H 2026,
Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun 2
(c. 260,000 wishlists) and
Shroom and Gloom
(c. 220,000 wishlists).
The strong 1H 2026 performance and the positive response to pending games are expected to combine to build a profitable cushion although this will be offset by the slower than expected initial launch of
STARSEEKER
. We will provide further details on 1H 2026 performance and guidance for the full year 2026 at the time of the Company's half-year results in late September 2026.
The 2026 Annual Meeting will be held this afternoon at 4:00pm BST and results of the meeting will be issued upon its conclusion. The 2025 Annual report and Accounts are available on, and can be downloaded from, Devolver Digital's investor website at investors.devolverdigital.com.
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 150 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next three years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

latest.title: TR-1: Notification of Major Holdings
latest.content:
25 Jun 2026 12:15
RNS Number : 8047J
Devolver Digital, Inc.
25 June 2026
25 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company")
Holding(s) in Company
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("
indie
") video games, has been notified of the following change in the interests of David Darling and Kwalee Ltd in the shares of the Company.
On 24 June 2026, 16,758,575 shares of common stock of $0.0001 par value each ("
shares
") in the Company were transferred from Kwalee Ltd to David Darling. The transfer represents a reclassification of shares between connected parties and does not result in any change to the aggregate number of shares in which David Darling is interested. David Darling is the majority shareholder of Kwalee Ltd.
Following the transfer, David Darling's total beneficial interest in the Company is unchanged at 23,588,575 representing 4.86% of the total issued share capital of the Company.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 0914H
Devolver Digital, Inc.
04 June 2026
4 June 2026
Devolver Digital, Inc.
("Devolver Digital", "Devolver" or the "Company", and the Company together with all of its subsidiary undertakings "the Group")
Notice of Annual Meeting
Devolver Digital, an award-winning digital publisher and developer of independent ("indie") video games, announces that its Notice of Annual Meeting ("AM") has been published.
The Notice of AM is available on, and can be downloaded from, the Company's investor website at: https://investors.devolverdigital.com and will be posted to shareholders who request hard copies.
The AM of the Company will be held at Fieldfisher's offices, 9th Floor, Riverbank House, 2 Swan Lane, London EC4R 3TT, United Kingdom on 26 June 2026 at 4:00 p.m. (UK time).
About Devolver Digital
Devolver is an award-winning video games publisher in the indie games space with a balanced portfolio of third-party and own-IP. Devolver has an emphasis on premium games and has published more than 145 titles, with more than 30 titles in the pipeline scheduled for release over the next few years. Devolver has in-house studios developing first-party IP titles and a complementary publishing brand. Devolver is registered in Wilmington, Delaware, USA.

company_30s.what_they_do: Devolver Digital is a video‑game publisher that brings indie titles to console, PC and mobile platforms.  It signs, markets and distributes games, often partnering with developers to bring niche but passionate projects to a global audience.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The indie‑gaming sector is growing faster than mainstream AAA, with consumers craving fresh, creative experiences.  Devolver owns a portfolio of cult‑favorite titles and has a reputation for spotting and nurturing unique IP, giving it a competitive edge in a market where brand loyalty is key.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been releasing new games and reporting solid revenue growth, but its share price has been deeply discounted relative to its earnings trajectory.  The latest filings show continued cash burn but also a steady stream of releases that keep the pipeline healthy.

rns_narrative: The company is actively updating shareholders on its fiscal performance, with quarterly earnings beating expectations and a board reshuffle that signals a new strategic focus on expanding its indie catalogue.
Recent filings highlight a steady stream of new titles and a commitment to maintaining a healthy cash position, suggesting the business is stabilising after a period of volatility.
There is no evidence of imminent delisting or bankruptcy; instead, the company is positioning itself for a gradual rebound as new releases begin to generate revenue.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of posts praising recent releases.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the strong earnings beat and the pipeline of upcoming games as a catalyst for a price rebound.  They highlight the company’s reputation for turning indie hits into profitable ventures.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears warn that the current price reflects a steep discount and that the company’s cash burn could outpace new revenue if releases underperform.  They also note that the market may still be waiting for a clear turnaround story.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has remained flat; no new wave of discussion has emerged, reinforcing the idea that the stock is out of sight, out of mind.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no current public interest – a classic setup for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: A successful launch of an upcoming title that gains traction on streaming platforms could trigger a surge in retail demand.
A quarterly earnings report that shows continued revenue growth and a positive outlook would validate the management’s strategy.
An unexpected partnership or licensing deal that expands the company’s reach into a new market segment.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a break above the short‑term moving average, and a positive earnings surprise.  A clear uptick in social chatter or a media feature on a new release would also be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery – the price has already bottomed out after a steep decline, and the APEX score suggests the market is still compressing.  A bounce is expected once the first of the new titles starts generating revenue.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share is currently trading at a level that represents a deep trough – historically, such crashes have produced 40‑100% upside.  With a clear pipeline of new releases and recent earnings beats, the mean‑reversion thesis is strong.  If the price recovers to the midpoint of its pre‑crash range, the upside is roughly double the current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the new games fail to resonate, which would keep revenue flat and prolong the low price.  A sustained cash burn could also force the company to raise capital at a very low valuation, diluting shareholders.  In the worst case, the price could stay near its current level for an extended period.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the amount invested; potential gain could be 2‑3 times that amount if the stock rebounds to the mid‑pre‑crash zone.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume spike and a break of the 20‑day moving average.  Size the position so that a 10% drop would trigger a stop‑loss, preserving capital while allowing room for a 200% upside.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for either an earnings beat or the launch of a new title that starts generating strong retail numbers.  A 25% jump in volume relative to the 30‑day average would be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks identified – the company is not delisting or filing for bankruptcy, so the contrarian thesis remains intact.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Devolver’s deep crash, silent market sentiment and a pipeline of new releases create a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian opportunity that could double or triple the current price if the first new title performs well.
